name: MURAL STRATIFICATION OF INTESTINAL TRACT
definition: bowel wall thickening with abnormal separation of bowel layers on cross-sectional imaging = layer of fat attenuation (-18 to -64 HU) in submucosa sandwiched between inner + outer hemorrhage secondary to ulcer crater surrounded by a rim of edema overlying submucosal lymphoid follicle
dominant_finding: "double halo / target" sign = trilaminar appearance during late arterial phase:
distribution: persistent enhancement of bowel wall / segmental arteries
discriminating_features: "double halo / target" sign = trilaminar appearance during late arterial phase:
nonenhanced interposed layer of (3) submucosa with various degrees of attenuation:

CECT: "double halo / target" sign = trilaminar appearance during late arterial phase:
nonenhanced interposed layer of (3) submucosa with various degrees of attenuation:
persistent enhancement of bowel wall / segmental arteries
